44054006.notes: Default canonical mapping for unspecified T2DM.

1551000119108.description: Type 2 diabetes mellitus with mild nonproliferative diabetic retinopathy without macular edema, unspecified eye
1551000119108.snomed_display: Nonproliferative diabetic retinopathy due to type II diabetes mellitus
1551000119108.source: SNOMED browser canonical mapping (per Brian); ACCESS CKM (is-a E11)
1551000119108.notes: Verified against SNOMED browser's official SNOMED→ICD-10-CM mapping by Brian on 2026-05-03.

157141000119108.description: Type 2 diabetes mellitus with diabetic nephropathy
157141000119108.snomed_display: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus (disorder)
157141000119108.notes: Proteinuria is the clinical hallmark of diabetic nephropathy.

90781000119102.description: Type 2 diabetes mellitus with other diabetic kidney complication
90781000119102.snomed_display: Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus (disorder)
90781000119102.source: SNOMED browser canonical mapping + cross-walk synonymy match (per Brian); ACCESS CKM (is-a E11)
90781000119102.notes: Verified by Brian on 2026-05-03. Distinct from proteinuria (E11.21) — microalbuminuria is an earlier-stage diabetic kidney finding.

368581000119106.description: Type 2 diabetes mellitus with diabetic neuropathy, unspecified
368581000119106.snomed_display: Neuropathy due to type 2 diabetes mellitus (disorder)

127013003.description: Type 2 diabetes mellitus with diabetic chronic kidney disease
127013003.snomed_display: Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus (disorder)
127013003.notes: Most general diabetic kidney complication code.

162864005.notes: Generic BMI-30+ obesity → unspecified obesity code.

408512008.description: Morbid (severe) obesity due to excess calories

414545008.source: SNOMED browser canonical mapping (per Brian); ICD-10-CM 2026
414545008.notes: I25.9 is the semantic match per the SNOMED browser. ACCESS CKM ValueSet explicitly enumerates I25.10-I25.119, I25.3, I25.41-I25.6 but NOT I25.9 (the unspecified parent). Per Brian's methodology decision (2026-05-03): we stay true to (a) the SNOMED browser's canonical SNOMED→ICD mapping and (b) the explicit ACCESS ValueSet as published. Conditions emitted with I25.9 will be filtered out by is_in_scope() — they don't reach the benchmark cohort. Documented in §Methods as a known consequence of strict scope adherence.

1501000119109.description: Type 2 diabetes mellitus with proliferative diabetic retinopathy without macular edema, unspecified eye
1501000119109.snomed_display: Proliferative diabetic retinopathy due to type II diabetes mellitus
1501000119109.notes: Verified by Brian — E11.3599 is the right choice. Alternatives like E11.351X (retinal detachment subtypes) are too specific to features not in the SNOMED display. Mirrors the nonproliferative companion entry 1551000119108 → E11.3299.

230690007.source: SNOMED browser crosswalk + ACCESS CKM (explicit) — Brian-verified 2026-05-03
230690007.notes: Verified by Brian — I63.9 is in the SNOMED browser crosswalk and is the best in-scope choice. I64 is also a possible mapping per browser but doesn't appear in other lookups Brian uses, so I63.9 wins.

60951000119105.description: Type 2 diabetes mellitus with other diabetic ophthalmic complication
60951000119105.snomed_display: Blindness due to type 2 diabetes mellitus (disorder)
60951000119105.source: ICD-10-CM ↔ SNOMED synonym match (per Brian) + ACCESS CKM (is-a E11) — Brian-verified 2026-05-03
60951000119105.notes: Verified by Brian — ICD-10-CM has a synonym match between E11.39 and this SNOMED concept that's quite exact.

97331000119101.description: Type 2 diabetes mellitus with unspecified diabetic retinopathy with macular edema
97331000119101.snomed_display: Macular edema and retinopathy due to type 2 diabetes mellitus (disorder)
97331000119101.notes: Verified by Brian — E11.311 is the most specific + best match. Alternatives with severity (E11.321 mild / E11.331 moderate / E11.341 severe / E11.351 proliferative) would be too specific given the SNOMED display doesn't specify retinopathy severity.
